mechanism_narrative: A persistently low UNRATE (below 5%) indicates a tight labor market that forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, reducing equity valuations and resulting in negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE is below 5%, the hypothesis is rejected. Also if the rank correlation between UNRATE level and forward returns is greater than 0 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.25%, monetary policy becomes restrictive and the borrowing costs for corporations and households rise sharply, triggering an economic slowdown that depresses corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: If the average forward 60-day return when FEDFUNDS > 5.25% is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the proportion of negative returns is less than 60%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in both HOUST (housing starts) and RSAFS (retail sales) signals a contraction in consumer spending and residential investment, which are key drivers of economic growth, leading to weaker corporate sales and negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: Both HOUST and RSAFS decline month-over-month
failure_condition: If in at least 50% of months where both series decline, the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is positive, the hypothesis is falsified. Also if the joint signal does not predict returns better than each series individually (rolling window IC test).

mechanism_narrative: During periods when UNRATE is rising (monthly increases) and FEDFUNDS remains elevated above 5%, the economy faces a stagflationary mix of rising unemployment and tight monetary policy, which severely depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
regime_hint: rising unemployment and tight monetary policy
failure_condition: If the average forward 60-day return when both conditions are met is not negative with a one-sided t-test p-value < 0.05, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 60%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO year-over-year growth exceeds 5%, it signals that industrial production is accelerating too rapidly, which creates supply bottlenecks and inflationary pressures that compel the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: If the subsequent 60-day return is positive in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO YoY > 5%, or if the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth as measured by RSAFS signals robust consumer demand that drives higher corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between RSAFS level and forward 20-day returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window, or if the average return in periods of above-median RSAFS is not higher than in below-median RSAFS (one-sided test p > 0.05), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in PAYEMS (nonfarm payroll employment) is a direct indicator of labor market weakness that immediately reduces consumer income and spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward 5-day returns as markets price in a softening economy.
failure_condition: If the average forward 5-day return in the five trading days following a negative payrolls print is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or if more than 50% of such instances yield positive returns, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment defined by CPIAUCSL year-over-year exceeding 3%, any increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is particularly harmful to equities because it raises real yields and discount rates while inflation persists, reducing the present value of future cash flows and leading to negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 3% and FEDFUNDS month-over-month increase
failure_condition: If the average forward 60-day return when both conditions are met is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p < 0.05) or the hit rate for negative returns is less than 60%, the hypothesis is rejected.
